steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a subject-verb agreement question. The subject is survey (singular), buried inside the long noun phrase “Her exhaustive chromosomal survey of plants in Silent Valley … (many of which are endangered).” The narrative is set in the 1970s, so past tense is required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "are": plural verb; cannot agree with the singular subject survey. Also wrong tense (present). ✗
  • "were": plural past-tense verb; still disagrees with the singular subject survey. ✗
  • "have been": plural present-perfect verb; disagrees with the singular subject survey. ✗
  • "was": singular past-tense verb; correctly agrees with the singular subject survey. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
